messages.title: SafeRoute AI — Group Project
messages.text: Aisha Rahman created group “SafeRoute AI — Group Project”
Hey everyone! Our group project is to build SafeRoute AI, a portable application that helps a transport company understand risky driving behaviour and predict unsafe trips.

text_entities.text: Hey everyone! Our group project is to build SafeRoute AI, a portable application that helps a transport company understand risky driving behaviour and predict unsafe trips.

messages.text: I read the brief. Phase 1 needs SQL Server storage, three useful SQL queries, an ETL pipeline to Python, EDA, visualisations and an interactive dashboard.
bro that is not phase 1 that is the whole company hahaha
Phase 2 adds feature engineering, model selection and optimisation, MLflow experiment tracking, plus a desktop prediction GUI.
Let’s split roles: Ben data engineer, Chloe data scientist, I handle dashboard and GUI, Aisha handles product coordination and business analysis. We can still review one another’s work.
Agreed. First milestone: working data pipeline and dashboard prototype by 21 August. Final demo is 25 September at 2 PM.

text_entities.text: First milestone: working data pipeline and dashboard prototype by 21 August. Final demo is 25 September at 2 PM.

messages.text: 25 sept sounds very far away. future us can panic 😂
yolo ship first debug during demo
absolutely not 😭 deadlines above are real pls
I’ll design the SQL schema and load the raw telematics CSV files by Friday 7 August, 6 PM. I’ll also draft the three complex queries.
Once Ben’s cleaned extract is ready, I’ll finish the EDA notebook and baseline model by 14 August. I need a data dictionary before I can confirm the features.
I can create the Power BI dashboard shell this week. Who is writing the user stories and product backlog?
I’ll own the user stories and initial product backlog. Draft due Wednesday 5 August. We need at least managers, administrators, and drivers represented.
Decision: use accident risk as the prediction target and compare logistic regression, random forest and XGBoost. Metrics should include recall and F1, not only accuracy.
Sounds good. The dataset is around seven million rows, so I’ll load it in chunks and index vehicle_id, trip_id and timestamp.
I uploaded the project brief and meeting notes to the shared drive. Please add your acceptance criteria to the backlog before tomorrow’s stand-up.
Dashboard plan: overview KPIs, risky behaviour trends, driver comparison, map of incidents, and filters for date and vehicle type. Prototype by 12 August.
Great. Please make sure a manager can answer: which behaviours contribute most to dangerous trips?
Blocker: the raw CSV has inconsistent timestamp formats and about 18% missing speed values. The SQL load will be late unless we agree on an imputation rule.
this is fine.gif
HAHA very helpful daniel thanks
Don’t impute speed blindly. Keep a missing-value flag, remove impossible readings, and compare median-by-road-type against dropping those rows. I can test this tomorrow morning.
Decision recorded. Ben, revise the ETL deadline to Monday 10 August noon. Chloe will send the missing-value recommendation by Saturday 2 PM.
Recommendation sent: retain rows, add speed_missing, and use road-type median for the dashboard only. For modelling, compare imputation inside cross-validation to avoid leakage.

ETL v1 is working and the database has 6.8 million valid rows. Query 1 identifies speeding hotspots; query 2 compares harsh braking by driver; query 3 still needs an owner to validate the business meaning.
I’ll validate query 3 with the manager user story by 11 August, 5 PM. Please send me the query and sample output.
Dashboard prototype is ready, but the incident map is blocked because we don’t have latitude and longitude in the cleaned table.
I excluded location fields because of nulls. I can add the valid coordinates to the view by tomorrow 3 PM.
EDA and baseline notebook are complete. Harsh braking frequency and overspeed duration are the strongest initial signals. Baseline F1 is 0.71.
Nice. I’ll connect those findings to the dashboard narrative. Someone should start the Phase 1 report and slides soon.
slides can be vibes right
vibes + one architecture diagram = distinction
haha funny. who is actually doing them?
I can probably start the report this weekend.
Reminder: our pipeline/dashboard milestone is Friday. Has anyone confirmed who owns the presentation slides and Scrum report?
I can design the slides if Aisha supplies the storyline by Thursday. Scrum report still has no confirmed owner.
Confirmed: I’ll send the storyline Thursday 20 August noon. Daniel owns the slides, first draft due 23 August. We still need someone for the Scrum report.
Milestone review: database and ETL done, EDA done, dashboard mostly done. The location filter is still buggy and the report has only an outline.
us saying mostly done when the report is one heading and a dream
seen by everyone. claimed by no one 💀
I’ll fix the location filter by Monday 24 August, 4 PM. I’m blocked until Ben republishes the coordinate view.
I’ll publish the corrected coordinate view tonight by 9 PM.
For Phase 2, I’ll run feature selection and tune random forest/XGBoost by 4 September. I’ll track parameters, metrics and artifacts in MLflow.
I’ll build the desktop prediction GUI after Chloe freezes the model interface. Target for working GUI: 11 September.
Final deliverables checklist: report under 20 pages, notebook, executable GUI, slides under 30 pages, product backlog, bi-weekly Scrum reports and individual peer evaluations.
Model tuning complete. XGBoost wins with F1 0.82 and recall 0.86. Need Daniel to confirm the input/output contract before I export the model.
I’ll confirm the GUI contract tomorrow before noon. Inputs: overspeed duration, harsh braking count, trip distance and weather category. Output: risk probability plus Low/Medium/High label.
GUI is one day late but working now. The packaged executable is 480 MB, so I need to reduce its size before submission.
I can investigate a smaller build, but I have not committed to a deadline yet.

Ten days to demo. Open items: shrink executable, complete final report, finish slides, rehearse 20-minute presentation, update Scrum records and collect peer evaluations. Please claim anything without an owner today.
ten days is basically two weeks if you round up
that is not how time works hahaha
serious pls: Ben shrink the executable by 18 Sept 6 PM. Daniel finish slides by 20 Sept noon. Chloe own final model appendix by 19 Sept. I will finish the report and book rehearsal for 22 Sept 4 PM.
roger 🫡 yolo but with assigned owners
